description: Register and execute text commands with a / prefix in chat messages

actors.name: User
actors.description: Any authenticated user who types slash commands in a chat message

actors.name: Platform
actors.description: The chat platform that parses messages and dispatches command execution

actors.name: Command Handler
actors.description: The registered callback or extension that executes the command logic

general: Command names must be unique across the platform; registering a duplicate command replaces or errors depending on configuration
Command names are lowercased and must contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens
Command execution is triggered only when the full message starts with /, followed by a registered command name
Commands with a required permission are hidden from users who lack that permission
If provides_preview is true, the platform calls the previewer function as the user types and shows up to 10 preview items
Selecting a preview item triggers executePreviewItem instead of the main executor
Command callbacks receive read-only access to the platform data; mutations go through the modify accessor
Commands may send ephemeral messages back to the invoking user without posting to the room
Unrecognized command names should surface a help hint rather than silently fail
Extensions and apps may register, enable, or disable commands dynamically at runtime

given: user sends a message starting with / followed by a registered command name
user has the required permission for the command (if any)
command_executed.result: Command handler is invoked with the command name, params, room context, and user context

given: user is typing a command whose provides_preview is true
user has not yet submitted the message
preview_shown.result: Platform calls the previewer and displays up to 10 inline preview items to the user

given: user selects one of the preview items
preview_item_selected.result: Platform calls executePreviewItem with the selected item instead of the main executor

given: user sends a message starting with / followed by a registered command name
command requires a permission the user does not hold
permission_denied.result: Command is not displayed to the user and execution is rejected

given: user sends a message starting with / followed by a name that is not a registered command
command_not_found.result: Platform surfaces a help message or ignores the unknown command

given: user invokes a command that is currently disabled by an extension or admin
command_disabled.result: Execution is blocked and an informational message may be shown to the user

errors.message: The command you entered was not found. Type /help to see available commands.
You do not have permission to use this command.

events.name: slash_command.executed
events.description: Fires when a registered slash command is successfully dispatched to its handler

events.name: slash_command.preview_requested
events.description: Fires when the platform requests inline preview results while a user is typing a command

events.name: slash_command.preview_item_selected
events.description: Fires when a user selects one of the inline preview items

events.name: slash_command.registered
events.description: Fires when a new slash command is registered by a handler or extension

events.name: slash_command.unregistered
events.description: Fires when a slash command is removed or disabled

related.reason: Extensions and bots use the plugin framework to register slash commands dynamically
Permission-gated commands rely on RBAC to determine access

goals.description: Parse, authorise, and dispatch slash commands from chat messages to registered handlers with permission enforcement and preview support

success_metrics.measurement: Permission-gated commands hidden from and rejected for unauthorised users / total attempts

human_checkpoints: before registering a system-level slash command that affects all users

invariants: command names must be unique across the platform
permission-required commands are hidden from users who lack the permission
unrecognised commands surface a help hint rather than silently failing

acceptance_tests.given: user without required permission types a permission-gated command
acceptance_tests.expect: SLASH_COMMAND_PERMISSION_DENIED and command not displayed to the user

acceptance_tests.given: command has provides_preview true and user is still typing
acceptance_tests.expect: up to 10 inline preview items displayed

capabilities.description: Register and unregister slash commands dynamically by extensions and bots
Call command preview functions while user types and display results inline

tradeoffs.reason: unknown commands show a help hint rather than an error to guide users to available commands
